body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-free Node.js JSON REST API for user signup/login and per-user private notes, backed by an on-disk store that survives restarts.

## Slicing
- On-disk store module (schema + user/session/note queries) against DB_PATH.
- Password hashing + opaque session token issuance/resolution.
- HTTP server with routing, JSON body parsing, and error handling.
- Endpoints: /signup, /login, /me, POST /notes, GET /notes with per-user isolation.
- package.json start script and .gitignore for the data dir; tests.

## Spec
Current state (verified 2026-07-21): the repository has no application code — `package.json` declares only `typecheck`/`lint`/`test` scripts and `main` is null. This Story introduces the whole service from scratch.

Approach — one cohesive HTTP service, no external web framework:

- **Server**: a single `node:http` server in `src/server.js`, routing on `(method, pathname)`. It reads `PORT` from the environment, defaulting to 3000, and exports a `createServer`/`start` factory so tests can bind an ephemeral port. Request bodies are read from the stream, size-capped, and `JSON.parse`d inside a try/catch — malformed JSON yields `400`. Every response is JSON with an appropriate status; unknown routes yield `404`; method mismatches `405`.
- **Persistence**: `node:sqlite` (the built-in `DatabaseSync`, stable on Node 22+/24) writing to an on-disk file at `data/app.db` (path overridable via `DB_PATH` for tests). Two tables: `users` (`id` INTEGER PK, `username` UNIQUE NOT NULL, `password_hash`, `password_salt`), and `notes` (`id` INTEGER PK, `user_id` FK NOT NULL, `title`, `body`, `created_at`). Sessions live in a `sessions` table (`token` PK, `user_id`, `created_at`) so bearer tokens also survive restart. Schema is created idempotently with `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` at startup. `node:sqlite` is chosen over better-sqlite3/sql.js to honour the no-external-dependency intent while keeping a real embedded on-disk store. If a target Node lacks `node:sqlite`, the store module is the single swap point.
- **Auth**: passwords hashed with `node:crypto` `scryptSync` + a per-user random salt; never stored in plaintext, never logged (rules/security-baseline.md). `/login` verifies with a constant-time compare and issues an opaque token from `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')` persisted in `sessions`. Protected routes resolve the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header to a user via the `sessions` table; a missing/invalid token yields `401`.
- **Isolation**: `GET /notes` and note creation are always scoped by the authenticated `user_id`, so one user can never read or write another's notes.
- **Store module** (`src/store.js`) wraps all SQL so route handlers stay persistence-agnostic and unit tests can target it directly.
- **npm start**: `"start": "node src/server.js"` added to `package.json`; `data/` gitignored.

## Changes
- `src/server.js` — creates
- `src/store.js` — creates
- `src/auth.js` — creates
- `src/router.js` — creates
- `test/api.test.js` — creates
- `test/store.test.js` — creates
- `package.json` — creates
- `.gitignore` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: Server boots and listens on the PORT env var, defaulting to 3000 when PORT is unset (the app reads process.env.PORT).
- [ ] AC-2: POST /signup with JSON {"username","password"} creates a user and responds 201 with JSON containing the new user id and username, and never echoes the password.
- [ ] AC-3: POST /signup with a duplicate username, or a missing/blank username or password, responds with a 4xx status and does not create a user.
- [ ] AC-4: POST /login with correct credentials responds 200 with JSON {"session": <opaque string>}; wrong credentials respond 401.
- [ ] AC-5: GET /me with a valid "Authorization: Bearer <session>" header responds 200 with the signed-in user id and username; with a missing or invalid token responds 401.
- [ ] AC-6: POST /notes with a valid session and JSON {"title","body"} responds 201 with the created note (including its id and owning user); without a valid session responds 401.
- [ ] AC-7: GET /notes with a valid session responds 200 with a JSON array of only that user's notes; a second user never sees the first user's notes.
- [ ] AC-8: Malformed JSON bodies and unknown routes/methods return sensible 4xx status codes with a JSON error body (no unhandled 500 crash).
- [ ] AC-9: Users, notes, and sessions persist across a full server restart: data written before a process exit is readable after a fresh boot against the same on-disk DB file.
- [ ] AC-10: package.json defines a "start" script that launches the server (node src/server.js).

## Verify
- node --test test/store.test.js (unit)
- node --test test/api.test.js (contract)
- npm start & sleep 1; curl -sf -X POST localhost:3000/signup -d '{"username":"a","password":"b"}' -H "content-type: application/json"; kill %1 (validate)

## Non-Goals
- No password reset, email verification, refresh tokens, or session expiry/revocation endpoints.
- No note update or delete endpoints (only create + list).
- No external web framework, ORM, or third-party runtime dependency.
- No HTTPS/TLS termination, rate limiting, or CORS handling (out of scope for this slice).

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Deliver the entire notes-API service as one cohesive, self-verifiable slice — server, persistence, and auth are inseparable (a: single capability, no parallelizable independent sibling).","estimated_test_files":2} -->

<!-- plan-story: 473606056d4eadd1 -->
title: JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes (ESM, no web framework, on-disk store)
